qa_pairs.question: What happens if I accidentally delete a file from my `~/nearline` directory on Béluga?
qa_pairs.answer: If you delete a file from `~/nearline` on Béluga, the tape copy will be retained for up to 60 days.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact to restore a file that was deleted from `~/nearline` on Béluga?
qa_pairs.answer: You should contact technical support to restore a deleted file from `~/nearline` on Béluga.

qa_pairs.question: What information is required when requesting to restore a deleted file from `~/nearline`?
qa_pairs.answer: When requesting a file restoration, you need to provide the full path for the file(s) and the desired version by date.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to keep a copy of the directory structure for my /nearline space?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important to retain a copy of the complete directory structure of your /nearline space because you will need the full path for the file(s) when requesting a restoration.

qa_pairs.question: How can I save a copy of my /nearline directory structure?
qa_pairs.answer: You can save a copy of your /nearline directory structure by running the command `ls -R > ~/nearline_contents.txt` from the `~/nearline/PROJECT` directory.

qa_pairs.question: From which directory should I run the `ls -R > ~/nearline_contents.txt` command to get a copy of my /nearline contents?
qa_pairs.answer: You should run the command `ls -R > ~/nearline_contents.txt` from the `~/nearline/PROJECT` directory.
